title: Crafting the Perfect Phish
description: Master the art of subject lines, body copy, and visual design to maximize click rates.

slides.title: The Subject Line: Make or Break 📨
slides.content:
# You Have 3 Seconds

The average user spends **3 seconds** deciding whether to open an email. The subject line is everything.

**The Psychology of a Good Subject Line:**

| Trigger | Example Subject Line | Weapon |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Urgency** | "ACTION REQUIRED: Your password expires in 2 hours" | Scarcity |
| **Fear** | "Unusual sign-in attempt detected on your account" | Fear/Amygdala |
| **Curiosity** | "Your 2025 Bonus Statement (Confidential)" | Scarcity/Curiosity |
| **Greed** | "You've been selected for a $50 Amazon gift card" | Reciprocity |
| **Familiarity** | "Re: Q4 Budget Discussion - Follow Up" | Consistency |
| **Authority** | "Message from the Office of the CFO" | Authority |

quizData.question: Subject: 'FW: Invoice #44532 - Payment Overdue'. Which psychological triggers are combined?
quizData.explanation: Correct. 'FW:' implies it's part of an ongoing conversation. 'Overdue' creates urgency. 'Invoice' implies financial authority.

slides.title: The Body: Keep It Simple, Stupid 📝
slides.content:
# Don't Write a Novel

Phishing emails are **not** marketing emails. You don't need to explain the product. You need **one action**.

**The Formula for a High-Conversion Phish Body:**

1.  **Context (1 sentence):** *"As part of our ongoing security upgrades..."*
2.  **The Problem (1 sentence):** *"We've detected that your multi-factor authentication settings are incomplete."*
3.  **The Consequence (1 sentence):** *"If not updated by 5 PM today, your VPN access will be temporarily suspended."*
4.  **The Call to Action (Button/Link):** **[ Update Security Settings ]**

**Golden Rule:** The user should **never** have to scroll to find the link. The link should be **above the fold**.

slides.title: Example: Effective Phish Body 🎯
slides.content: Subject: Important: VPN Authentication Update Required
slides.codeSnippet:
Dear [Employee Name],

As part of our migration to Azure AD, we are requiring all employees to re-validate their VPN tokens. This is a one-time process.

Accounts that have not been validated by **5:00 PM EST today** will be temporarily locked out of the corporate network until validated by the Help Desk (24-48 hour turnaround).

Please complete the validation now:

[ Validate VPN Access ](https://vpn-acmecorp-auth.com)

Thank you,
IT Infrastructure Team
*This is an automated message. Please do not reply.*

slides.title: Visual Design & Branding 🎨
slides.content:
## Cloning the Look and Feel

**Nothing screams 'SCAM' like a plain text email with a blurry logo.**

**Pro Techniques:**
1.  **Steal the Real Email:** Sign up for the service you are impersonating. Get their **actual** welcome email. Right-click -> Inspect Element. Copy the **HTML structure**.
2.  **Host Images Remotely:** Do NOT embed images as attachments. Host them on your phishing server or use legitimate image URLs (e.g., pulling the real Microsoft logo from Microsoft's own CDN). This improves deliverability.
3.  **Match the Footer:** Real companies have 'Unsubscribe' links and privacy policies in the footer. **Add them.** Use `#` as the link target. It adds **Verisimilitude**.

quizData.question: Why is it effective to include a fake 'Unsubscribe' link and a 'Privacy Policy' link in the footer of a phishing email?

options.text: It makes the email look more legitimate and bypasses spam filters
It tricks the user into clicking more links

quizData.explanation: Correct. Legitimate marketing emails always have these. Their presence reduces suspicion and some spam filters flag emails that lack a physical address or unsubscribe link.

slides.title: Grammar and Spelling Check 🧐
slides.content:
# The Myth of 'Bad Grammar is a Filter'

**Old Wisdom:** "Phishing emails have bad spelling to filter out smart people."
**Modern Reality:** AI tools like ChatGPT write **flawless** English.

**BUT...** Sometimes a **small, deliberate error** can increase rapport.
- *"Sorry for the typo, typing this on my phone."* (Humanizes the sender).

**The Real Risk:** Obvious **Grammar Nazi Triggers** (Your/You're, There/Their/They're). If you use these wrong, you lose the educated targets. Use **Grammarly** or AI to proofread **EVERY** phish you send.

slides.title: Personalization Tokens 🔖
slides.content:
# Dear [FIRSTNAME]

Modern phishing frameworks (like **GoPhish** or **Evilginx**) support mail merge.

**Instead of:** *"Dear User,"*
**Use:** *"Dear Susan,"*

**How to get the data:**
- Scrape LinkedIn for the email format (`susan.miller@company.com`).
- Use a tool like **Hunter.io** to verify the format.
- Upload a CSV with `First Name, Last Name, Email` to your phishing platform.

**Result:** Open rates **increase by 29%** when the email includes the recipient's first name. (Source: Campaign Monitor).
